title: Make column locking a property that can be set by automations
excerpt:
Add the ability to set criteria (much like automation recipe) to a column for if it will be locked or not. heck it could just be a property we can automate too. 
When status changes to status… 
…disable BUTTON button. 
……

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
Add the ability to set criteria (much like automation recipe) to a column for if it will be locked or not. heck it could just be a property we can automate too.

When status changes to status…
…disable BUTTON button.
…lock COLUMN.

When number is greater than VALUE …
…lock COLUMN

posts.author: jailynnlucidday (Jai-Lynn Hoops)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is really interesting!

I am actually very curious what brought up this idea. I never thought of it but I could see this being a good added permission.

If you are using a specific workflow and want to lock down certain permissions for you and your team though I would suggest taking a look at the Workflow App! It’s in monday.com’s App Marketplace and allows for a little more controlled workflow and permissions.

Hope this helps!

posts.author: Shaz19 (sharon simmons)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Jai-Lynn I realise its a while since you posted but I wanted to add my use case. We have a timeline column used to estimate when each task in the project will be completed, using a one week range. As we all know nothing goes to plan so there is a second column where the task owner updates the Actual timeline week when the task is completed. We want the initial date to stay put so we can see any slippage and understand the root cause, by locking the column assignees can not accidently update the wrong timeline dates. I’d like to be able to lock this column automatically as soon as all the timelines for each task are completed.
